pending.body: Preserve journal-terminal outcomes before stale-owner recovery in `run resume`/`run stop`. `src/cli/run.rs:2117-2132`, `src/cli/run.rs:2263-2277`, and `src/cli/run.rs:2349-2363` now unconditionally call `recover_stale_running_snapshot` once the writer lease is claimed. That bypasses the existing journal-first reconciliation in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:1449-1504`, which is the code that distinguishes 'snapshot write missed a durable run_failed' from 'snapshot write missed a durable run_completed'. If the previous orchestrator already appended `run_completed` or `run_failed` and only the snapshot write failed, these new CLI paths will rewrite the run to `Failed` with an interruption/cancellation summary, and `run resume` can then restart a run that should have stayed terminal.

Make status reconciliation attempt-aware before trusting terminal journal events. The updated `reconcile_snapshot_status` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:27-40` now filters by `run_id`, but resumed attempts reuse the same run id (`tests/unit/workflow_engine_test.rs:3792-3800`). A live resumed run therefore still has an older `run_failed` for that same `run_id`, and `handle_status` (`src/cli/run.rs:5744-5749`) will incorrectly patch the current Running snapshot to Failed/Completed even though the resumed attempt is still active. The reconciliation needs to ignore terminal events that occurred before the most recent `run_started`/`run_resumed` boundary for the current attempt.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 10 -> Plan and Implement
